provenance.source_corpus: Transplanting Following Non-Native Plant Control in Rocky Mountain Foothills Fescue Grassland Restoration

source_spans.text: Transplant survival was improved by glyphosate application to control non-native plants prior to planting.
Transplant survival was improved by glyphosate application to control non-native plants prior to planting.
Glyphosate reduced non-native cover the most while cutting had no effect.
grass transplant flowering was significantly lower in treatments where vegetation had been cut prior to planting.
Transplanting was effective for increasing native cover and species richness although high winter mortality reduced this effect.

Transplanting was effective for increasing native cover and species richness although high winter mortality reduced this effect.
survival was higher for larger container stock.
